title: Appendix Ferra1.2: Universal Coordination Constants for Ordered and Disordered Phases
description: Within the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT), the fundamental error-scaling exponent β = 2/3 ≈ 0.67 and the minimal coordination constant κ_c = 1/3 give rise, through elementary operations (summation of geometric series), to two new universal constants: S_odd = 6/5 = 1.2 and S_even = 4/5 = 0.8. These constants represent the limiting contributions of 'unidirectional' (odd hierarchical levels) and 'alternating' (even levels) correlations in any hierarchical coordination system. The three numbers β, S_odd and S_even form a closed cyclically consistent system: S_odd + S_even = 2 and S_odd/S_even = 1/β = 3/2. We discuss the physical interpretation of these constants in ferromagnets, superfluids, genomic sequences, gravitational phenomena, fractal dislocation structures, gel systems, spin centers in carbon films, photoluminescence quenching, and catalytic properties. A remarkable approximate relation with π is also noted: S_odd·φ² ≈ π, where φ is the golden ratio. Additionally, the same constants emerge in the asymptotic distribution of prime numbers, providing an independent, quantitative verification of YUCT: the theoretical correction to Rosser's formula for the n-th prime is -(S_even/2)·n^{1-β}·ln n. The existence of such a self-consistent structure demonstrates the predictive power of YUCT and provides a new quantitative language for describing order–disorder transitions across all scales. This Appendix is part of the larger Yakushev's Law of Coordination framework (Yakushev's Law v2.5.MOD).

related_identifiers.label: Master YUCT v7.0 Treatise — Yakushev's Law of Coordination (DOI)

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, JA, KO, AR) of Appendix Ferra1.2: 'Universal Coordination Constants for Ordered and Disordered Phases'. Each language is provided in two formats: PDF (compiled ready-to-read) and TEX (source code for LaTeX compilation). This Appendix is part of the larger Yakushev's Law of Coordination framework (Yakushev's Law v2.5.MOD). The root main file is the English version, while the other languages are full translations of the same content. The translations were produced using LuaLaTeX with system fonts: English (Times New Roman), Russian (Times New Roman), German (Times New Roman), Chinese (SimSun/Yu Mincho), Japanese (Yu Gothic UI/Yu Mincho), Korean (Noto Sans KR/Noto Serif KR), Arabic (Amiri/Arabic Typesetting).

appendix_metadata.appendix_title: Universal Coordination Constants for Ordered and Disordered Phases

derived_predictions.prediction_ferromagnets: S_odd = 1.2 appears as universal enhancement factor for magnetization in Fe, Co, Ni
derived_predictions.prediction_genomic: ratio of unidirectional to alternating dinucleotides → 3/2 in coding regions of bacteria
derived_predictions.prediction_gravitational_gene_expression: coherent/incoherent gene response ratio → 1.5 in hypergravity conditions (NASA GeneLab)
derived_predictions.prediction_fractal_dislocations: dislocation distribution exponent 1.5, stress fluctuations exponent 1.2
derived_predictions.prediction_gel_systems: fractal dimension of silica gels → 2.0 = S_odd + S_even
derived_predictions.prediction_spin_centers: two distinct paramagnetic centers with g-factors, linewidth narrowing ratio ∼ 1.5
derived_predictions.prediction_photoluminescence: quenching sphere diameter ratio CD-180/CD-150 = 1.171 ≈ 1.2 (2.5% deviation)
derived_predictions.prediction_catalysis: sp²/sp³ ratio correlates with catalytic activity, optimal defect rings (5-5-8) match S_odd and S_even
derived_predictions.prediction_metal_clusters: FeC₆ magnetic moments → 1.85 ≈ S_odd + S_even = 2.0 and 1.5×1.2 = 1.8
derived_predictions.prediction_prime_numbers: Rosser's formula correction: -(S_even/2)·n^{1-β}·ln n, maximal error reduced from 2.3% to 0.012%

theoretical_ingredients.universal_error_law: ε = κ_c α (ln K_eff)^β with β = 2/3 ≈ 0.67
theoretical_ingredients.YPSDC_protocol: offline dictionary sharing + online index transmission
theoretical_ingredients.algebraic_loop_derivation: sums of geometric series with β=2/3
theoretical_ingredients.mass_ladder_scaling: ln(m/m_e) = N_f ln q with σ = 0.20 scatter
theoretical_ingredients.prime_correction_derivation: from algebraic loop and YUCT error law

notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of Appendix Ferra1.2: 'Universal Coordination Constants for Ordered and Disordered Phases', version 2.1. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, JA, KO, AR) is provided in two formats: PDF (compiled, ready-to-read) and TEX (LaTeX source code). This appendix is part of the larger Yakushev's Law of Coordination framework (Yakushev's Law v2.5.MOD). The English version is the original reference. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'. All multilingual metadata, fundamental constants (β=2/3≈0.67, S_odd=1.2, S_even=0.8, σ=0.20, π_YUCT≈3.1416407865), and ten falsifiable predictions are explicitly declared in the 'custom' block; the primary metadata language is English. The DOI for this appendix is 10.5281/zenodo.18444598, while the master YUCT treatise (Yakushev's Law of Coordination) uses 10.5281/zenodo.18444599. All translations were compiled and verified with LuaLaTeX using system fonts appropriate for each script.
